item.title: Continuous Integration and Continuous Deployment (CI/CD)
item.description:
What is CI/CD?

CI/CD (Continuous Integration / Continuous Deployment) is a practice of automating software building, testing, and deployment.

Continuous Integration (CI)

CI is a practice where developers frequently (several times a day) merge their changes into a shared repository. Each merge is automatically built and tested.

Benefits of CI:

  • Early bug detection
  • Fast feedback
  • Reduced integration risks

Continuous Deployment (CD)

CD is a practice of automatically deploying every change that passes tests to the production environment.

CI/CD Tools

  • GitHub Actions — built-in in GitHub
  • GitLab CI/CD — in GitLab
  • Jenkins — popular open-source tool
  • Travis CI — cloud CI service

Conclusion

CI/CD helps teams deliver software to users faster and more reliably.

item.title: Creating Presentations
item.description:
Presentation Tools

  • PowerPoint / LibreOffice Impress — classic editors
  • Reveal.js — HTML presentations built into Hugo
  • LaTeX Beamer — professional academic presentations
  • Canva — online service with templates
  • Google Slides — cloud tool

Good Presentation Rules

Text

  • No more than 10 lines per slide
  • Large font (at least 24pt)
  • Bullet points, not full sentences

Visual Design

  • Consistent style across all slides
  • Contrasting background and text colors
  • Use images and diagrams
  • Don’t overuse animation

Structure

  • Title slide (topic, author, date)
  • Table of contents slide
  • Main slides (5-7)
  • Conclusion slide

Conclusion

A good presentation is when the audience follows your story, not reads your slides.

item.title: Deploying a Bilingual Site on GitHub
item.description:
Prerequisites

To host your site on GitHub Pages, you will need:

  • A GitHub repository
  • A Hugo site ready for building
  • Basic Git skills

Build and Deploy

The deployment process consists of several steps:

  1. Install Hugo and create your site
  2. Configure bilingual support
  3. Build the site: hugo --gc --minify
  4. Copy to docs/: cp -r public docs
  5. Add .nojekyll: touch docs/.nojekyll
  6. Commit and push: git push origin main

GitHub Pages Settings

In your repository settings (Settings → Pages):

  • Source: Deploy from a branch
  • Branch: main, folder: /docs

Your site will be available at: https://username.github.io/my-academic-site/

Bilingual Site Features

When using two languages:

  • Primary language (RU) is served at the root /
  • Secondary language (EN) is at /en/
  • A redirect from /en/ to /en/home/ may be needed
  • A language switcher is placed in the header

GitHub Pages Benefits

  • Free hosting
  • Automatic HTTPS
  • Simple git push deployment
  • Full version control

item.title: Lightweight Markup Languages. Markdown
item.description:
What is Markdown?

Markdown is a lightweight markup language created by John Gruber in 2004. It allows formatting text using simple syntax and converting it to HTML.

Basic Syntax

Headings

# Heading 1
## Heading 2
### Heading 3

Lists

- Item 1
- Item 2
- Subitem
1. First
2. Second

Links and Images

[Link text](https://example.com)
![Alt text](image.png)

Applications

Markdown is widely used in:

  • Project documentation (README.md)
  • Blogs and static sites (Hugo, Jekyll)
  • Knowledge management systems (Obsidian, Notion)
  • Messengers (Telegram, Discord)
  • Developer platforms (GitHub, GitLab)

Conclusion

Markdown is a simple and convenient markup language that has become the standard for IT documentation.

item.title: Markup Languages. LaTeX
item.description:
What is LaTeX?

LaTeX is a document preparation system based on the TeX language. It is widely used in academia for preparing scientific publications, theses, and reports.

Benefits of LaTeX

  • Professional typesetting quality
  • Automatic numbering of equations, tables, and figures
  • Convenient bibliography management (BibTeX)
  • Complex mathematical formula support
  • Git-friendly (plain text format)
  • Free and cross-platform

Document Example

\documentclass{article}
\title{My First Paper}
\author{Yana Kulazhenkova}
\begin{document}
\maketitle
\section{Introduction}
Introduction text...
\end{document}

Formulas in LaTeX

LaTeX allows creating beautiful mathematical formulas:

  • $E = mc^2$ — inline formula
  • $$\int_0^\infty e^{-x^2} dx = \frac{\sqrt{\pi}}{2}$$ — displayed formula

Conclusion

LaTeX is the standard for scientific publications. Mastering it allows you to format any document professionally.

item.title: Report Formatting
item.description:
Report Structure

Any scientific or technical report should contain the following sections:

  1. Title page — work title, author, date
  2. Introduction — objective, relevance
  3. Main part — execution description
  4. Results — obtained data and analysis
  5. Conclusion — findings
  6. References — sources used

Formatting Requirements

  • Font: Times New Roman, 14pt
  • Line spacing: 1.5
  • Margins: left 30mm, right 10mm, top/bottom 20mm
  • Page numbering: bottom center
  • Figures and tables must have captions
  • Formulas are numbered in parentheses

Conclusion

Proper report formatting is an important part of the learning process. It teaches accuracy and discipline.

item.title: Version Control. Git
item.description:
What is Git?

Git is a distributed version control system that tracks changes in files and coordinates work among multiple people.

Key Concepts

  • Repository — storage for files and their change history
  • Commit — saving changes with a description
  • Branch — a separate line of development
  • Merge — combining changes from different branches
  • Conflict — when changes in the same file part contradict each other

Basic Commands

Command Description
git init Create a new repository
git clone Copy a remote repository
git add Add files to the index
git commit Create a commit
git push Send changes to server
git pull Fetch changes from server
git branch Manage branches
git merge Merge branches

Conclusion

Git is an essential tool for any developer. It simplifies collaboration and helps prevent data loss.
